settings.toneDescription: Confident, grounded, place-rooted. Northwind Trails writes about expeditions with deep respect for the land. The voice is warm but practical — never breezy, never marketing-glib.
settings.targetAudience: Adventure enthusiasts considering booking an expedition. Editorial readers who expect substance, not hype. Mostly UK & European, often returning customers familiar with the brand.
settings.styleGuidelines: Use active voice. Lead with concrete sensory detail. Be specific about places, conditions, expertise. Use first-person when describing guide experience. Cite specific years, regions, mountain names.
settings.avoidPatterns: Informal colloquialism. Generic marketing phrasing. Over-use of exclamation marks. Vague claims about 'adventure' or 'experience' without specifics. Corporate buzzwords.
